{"type":"item.completed","item":{"id":"item_0","type":"agent_message","text":"Break the import from `src/domain/billing.ts` to `src/presentation/invoice-view.ts`.

Why:

- It is part of the 3-file cycle:
  `billing-flow.ts -> billing.ts -> invoice-view.ts -> billing-flow.ts`
- It is also the explicit layer violation:
  domain imports presentation, which inverts the intended architecture.
- Breaking this edge fixes both the high-severity circular dependency and the medium-severity domain-to-presentation boundary violation.

Best fix: move whatever `billing.ts` needs from `invoice-view.ts` into a neutral module or a domain-owned type/interface. For example:

- If `billing.ts` only needs a type from `invoice-view.ts`, convert it to `import type` if safe.
- If it needs formatting/rendering behavior, move that behavior out of presentation or invert it behind a domain/application interface.
- Presentation should depend on domain, not the other way around.

I would not start by breaking the `billing-flow.ts` import unless inspection shows it is obviously type-only. The domain-to-presentation import is the architectural root of the cycle, so removing it gives the cleanest fix."}}

per_question.question: Did the agent correctly identify the cycle (domain → presentation → application → domain)?
per_question.reasoning: The agent correctly identified the 3-file cycle involving billing.ts (domain), invoice-view.ts (presentation), and billing-flow.ts (application). The cycle is described as `billing-flow.ts -> billing.ts -> invoice-view.ts -> billing-flow.ts`, which traces application → domain → presentation → application — the same cycle as the expected domain → presentation → application → domain, just starting from a different node. All three expected files are referenced, and the agent explicitly calls out the domain-to-presentation layer violation as the architectural root.

per_question.question: Did the agent's fix preserve the dependency direction (lower-layer should not depend on higher)?
per_question.reasoning: The agent's proposed fix correctly preserves layer direction by targeting the domain → presentation import as the one to break, explicitly stating 'Presentation should depend on domain, not the other way around.' The remediation suggestions (move shared types to a neutral module, invert behavior behind a domain/application interface, use `import type` if applicable) all respect the rule that lower layers (domain) must not depend on higher layers (presentation/application). The reasoning explicitly rejects breaking the application → domain edge because that would not address the architectural inversion.
